Who I Am

About Me

I am Kumari Akta, a Biotechnology undergraduate driven by a deep intellectual curiosity about living systems and a conviction that the most meaningful advances in human health will come from minds equally comfortable at the bench and behind a dataset. From the earliest stages of my education, I have been drawn not just to how biology works, but to why it fails — and how science can intervene.

My academic focus gravitates toward the intersection of molecular biology, genomics, and computational analysis. I am fascinated by the idea that hidden within biological sequences, protein structures, and cellular pathways are patterns waiting to be decoded — patterns that could one day inform how diseases are diagnosed, how drugs are designed, and how healthcare is personalised at the individual level.

Beyond the laboratory, I am deeply interested in the translational dimension of biotechnology — understanding how fundamental research moves from discovery to real-world application. I follow developments in precision medicine, gene editing, and bioprocess innovation closely, and I am committed to developing the interdisciplinary literacy that modern life sciences demand.

I approach my development with intention: building technical depth, seeking meaningful exposure to research environments, and cultivating the scientific rigour necessary to contribute at the frontier of healthcare innovation and life sciences research. I am early in this journey — and entirely committed to where it leads.

Scientific Curiosity
Driven by a genuine desire to understand biological systems at their deepest level.
Intentional Growth
Building expertise with purpose — seeking depth over breadth at every stage.
Translational Thinking
Interested in how research moves from discovery to real-world healthcare impact.
Forward-Looking
Committed to contributing at the frontier of genomics and precision medicine.

Senior Secondary — Science Stream
Air Force School Viman Nagar, Pune
Completed higher secondary education with a strong foundation in Physics, Chemistry, and Biology — the classical scientific trinity that ignited a sustained interest in life sciences and the natural world. Graduated from one of the prestigious Air Force Schools, nurturing discipline, analytical thinking, and academic rigour.

Field Experience

Internship & Training

CSIR-IIIM Jammu
Council of Scientific & Industrial Research  ·  2025
Participated in an intensive hands-on training program at CSIR — Indian Institute of Integrative Medicine, one of India's foremost CSIR laboratories. Acquired practical exposure to upstream and downstream bioprocessing, fermentation technology, bioreactor operations, and standard bioproduction techniques. Gained an understanding of how laboratory-scale research is scaled for industrial and pharmaceutical applications, and was introduced to quality control protocols within a GMP-oriented research environment.
